title: Events, Observability, Capacity, Inventory, and Cost Control · Go Semantics Case: Request Trace Audit
summary: Operators have high-cardinality object-key metrics, duplicate unordered events, incomplete request traces, no inventory reconciliation, and no alert tied to a user or cost outcome. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the request trace audit increment in storage telemetry, inventory, capacity, and cost model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Correlate application operation, AWS request IDs, CloudTrail data events where justified, access logs, CloudFront logs, and stakeholder result without secrets.
Consume S3 notifications through supported destinations with duplicate, ordering, filtering, recursion, schema, delay, and lost-consumer recovery limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
